slices.name: _run

19 | def _run(args: list, cwd: str = _CWD) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess:
  20 |     return subprocess.run(
  21 |         [sys.executable, _VALIDATOR] + args,
  22 |         cwd=cwd,
  23 |         capture_output=True,
  24 |         text=True,
  25 |     )

slices.name: _write_bundle

28 | def _write_bundle(directory: pathlib.Path, data: dict, name: str = "bundle.json") -> pathlib.Path:
  29 |     path = directory / name
  30 |     path.write_text(json.dumps(data), encoding="utf-8")
  31 |     return path

  41 |     """Return a minimal valid synthetic bundle for testing."""
  42 |     base = {

  44 |             "bundle_schema_version": "test-bundle-v1",
  45 |             "deterministic": True,
  46 |             "stats": {"bundled": 1, "skipped": 0, "errors": 0},
  47 |         },
  48 |         "layer_1_8_entry_points": ["main.py"],
  49 |         "layer_2_intelligence": [{"path": "main.py", "import_graph": ["json", "pathlib"]}],
  50 |         "layer_x_uncertainties": {"dynamic_behaviors": [], "syntax_errors": []},
  51 |         "system_architecture_context": {
  52 |             "system_contracts": ["Assertion constraint: result is not None"],
  53 |             "external_dependencies": {"stdlib": ["json", "pathlib"], "third_party": []},
  54 |         },
  55 |     }
  56 |     base.update(overrides)
  57 |     return base

slices.name: TestHelpAndBasicCLI
slices.code:
70 | class TestHelpAndBasicCLI(unittest.TestCase):
  71 |     def test_help_exits_zero(self):
  72 |         result = _run(["--help"])
  73 |         self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0)
  74 |         self.assertIn("--bundle", result.stdout)
  75 |         self.assertIn("--config", result.stdout)
  76 |         self.assertIn("--out", result.stdout)
  77 |         self.assertIn("--markdown-out", result.stdout)
  78 | 
  79 |     def test_missing_bundle_exits_nonzero(self):
  80 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
  81 |             out = pathlib.Path(td) / "report.json"
  82 |             result = _run(["--out", str(out)])
  83 |             self.assertNotEqual(result.returncode, 0)
  84 | 
  85 |     def test_nonexistent_bundle_exits_nonzero(self):
  86 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
  87 |             out = pathlib.Path(td) / "report.json"
  88 |             result = _run(["--bundle", "no_such_file.json", "--out", str(out)])
  89 |             self.assertNotEqual(result.returncode, 0)
  90 |             self.assertIn("ERROR", result.stdout + result.stderr)
  91 | 
  92 |     def test_malformed_json_bundle_exits_nonzero(self):
  93 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
  94 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
  95 |             bad = tmp / "bad.json"
  96 |             bad.write_text("{ not valid json }", encoding="utf-8")
  97 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
  98 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bad), "--out", str(out)])
  99 |             self.assertNotEqual(result.returncode, 0)

slices.name: TestPassingSyntheticBundle
slices.code:
106 | class TestPassingSyntheticBundle(unittest.TestCase):
 107 |     def test_empty_config_passes(self):
 108 |         """No rules fired when config has no expectations."""
 109 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 110 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 111 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 112 |             cfg = _write_config(tmp, _minimal_config())
 113 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 114 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--config", str(cfg), "--out", str(out)])
 115 |             self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)
 116 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 117 |             self.assertEqual(data["status"], "PASS")
 118 |             self.assertEqual(data["findings_count"], 0)
 119 | 
 120 |     def test_no_config_passes(self):
 121 |         """Running without --config must still produce a valid PASS report."""
 122 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 123 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 124 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 125 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 126 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--out", str(out)])
 127 |             self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)
 128 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 129 |             self.assertEqual(data["status"], "PASS")
 130 | 
 131 |     def test_report_contains_expected_keys(self):
 132 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 133 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 134 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 135 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 136 |             _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--out", str(out)])
 137 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 138 |             for key in ("status", "bundle", "findings_count", "findings", "severity_counts"):
 139 |                 self.assertIn(key, data, f"Missing key: {key}")
 140 | 
 141 |     def test_sample_fixture_with_python_project_config(self):
 142 |         """The sample_bundle.json fixture must produce a valid report with no crash."""
 143 |         self.assertTrue(_SAMPLE_BUNDLE.exists(), f"Sample bundle missing: {_SAMPLE_BUNDLE}")
 144 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 145 |             out = pathlib.Path(td) / "report.json"
 146 |             result = _run([
 147 |                 "--bundle", str(_SAMPLE_BUNDLE),
 148 |                 "--config", str(_EXAMPLES / "python_project.json"),
 149 |                 "--profile", "default",
 150 |                 "--out", str(out),
 151 |             ])
 152 |             # We don't assert PASS/FAIL — the sample fixture may trigger WARN/FAIL.
 153 |             # We only assert it completes cleanly (exit 0 or 2), not 1 (invocation error).
 154 |             self.assertIn(result.returncode, (0, 2), result.stderr)
 155 |             self.assertTrue(out.exists())
 156 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 157 |             self.assertIn(data["status"], ("PASS", "WARN", "FAIL"))

164 | class TestRequiredPathsAndExts(unittest.TestCase):

 171 |             ))
 172 |             out = tmp / "report.json"

slices.name: TestPluginFailureIsolation
slices.code:
438 | class TestPluginFailureIsolation(unittest.TestCase):
 439 |     def _write_plugin(self, directory: pathlib.Path, code: str, name: str = "plugin.py") -> pathlib.Path:
 440 |         path = directory / name
 441 |         path.write_text(code, encoding="utf-8")
 442 |         return path
 443 | 
 444 |     def test_crashing_plugin_becomes_warn_not_crash(self):
 445 |         """A plugin that raises on import or execution must become a WARN finding, not a hard crash."""
 446 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 447 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 448 |             plugin = self._write_plugin(tmp, "def run_checks(bundle, config):\n    raise RuntimeError('plugin exploded')\n")
 449 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 450 |             cfg = _write_config(tmp, {
 451 |                 "schema_version": "1.0",
 452 |                 "plugins": [str(plugin)],
 453 |                 "architecture_expectations": {},
 454 |                 "risk_rules": {},
 455 |             })
 456 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 457 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--config", str(cfg), "--out", str(out)])
 458 |             # Must NOT exit 1 (invocation error); plugin failure is not a crash
 459 |             self.assertIn(result.returncode, (0, 2), result.stderr)
 460 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 461 |             ids = [f["id"] for f in data["findings"]]
 462 |             self.assertIn("PLUGIN-ERROR", ids)
 463 |             # Severity must be WARN, not FAIL
 464 |             severities = {f["id"]: f["severity"] for f in data["findings"]}
 465 |             self.assertEqual(severities.get("PLUGIN-ERROR"), "WARN")
 466 | 
 467 |     def test_working_plugin_findings_included(self):
 468 |         """A well-behaved plugin that returns findings must have them in the report."""
 469 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 470 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 471 |             plugin = self._write_plugin(tmp,
 472 |                 "def run_checks(bundle, config):\n"
 473 |                 "    return [{'id': 'P001', 'severity': 'INFO', 'message': 'plugin ran'}]\n"
 474 |             )
 475 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 476 |             cfg = _write_config(tmp, {
 477 |                 "schema_version": "1.0",
 478 |                 "plugins": [str(plugin)],
 479 |                 "architecture_expectations": {},
 480 |                 "risk_rules": {},
 481 |             })
 482 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 483 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--config", str(cfg), "--out", str(out)])
 484 |             self.assertEqual(result.returncode, 0, result.stderr)
 485 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 486 |             ids = [f["id"] for f in data["findings"]]
 487 |             self.assertIn("P001", ids)
 488 | 
 489 |     def test_nonexistent_plugin_path_becomes_warn(self):
 490 |         with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as td:
 491 |             tmp = pathlib.Path(td)
 492 |             bundle = _write_bundle(tmp, _minimal_bundle())
 493 |             cfg = _write_config(tmp, {
 494 |                 "schema_version": "1.0",
 495 |                 "plugins": ["nonexistent_plugin_xyz.py"],
 496 |                 "architecture_expectations": {},
 497 |                 "risk_rules": {},
 498 |             })
 499 |             out = tmp / "report.json"
 500 |             result = _run(["--bundle", str(bundle), "--config", str(cfg), "--out", str(out)])
 501 |             self.assertIn(result.returncode, (0, 2))
 502 |             data = json.loads(out.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
 503 |             ids = [f["id"] for f in data["findings"]]
 504 |             self.assertIn("PLUGIN-ERROR", ids)

 539 |             self.assertIn("Findings", content)
 540 |             self.assertIn("R-AV008a", content)
